revision.text:
[[ja:赤坂上之山神社]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] | name = Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine | image = File:Akasakakaminoyama-jinja1.jpg | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kawachinagano|en|Kawachinagano|qid=Q819572|lt=Kawachinagano|1=Kawachinagano}}, Mikanodai | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 25 | Latitude seconds = 38.4 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 34 | Longitude seconds = 44.7 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP-27 | Map = Japan Osaka | Enshrined deities = {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | Established = Traditionally, Shingon Kei'un 1 (767) | Main hall = | alternate_name = Shōhachimangū (former name)
Kami-no-Miya (common name) | Pilgrimage sites = | Annual festival = | Sacred rites = | location = {{ill|Kawachinagano|qid=Q819572|1=Kawachinagano}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.42733333333334|N|135.57908333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine''' (赤坂上之山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|en|Kawachinagano|qid=Q819572|lt=Kawachinagano|1=Kawachinagano}}, Osaka Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. ==Overview== [[Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine]] is a regional Sōja shrine. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == Enshrined Deities == The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|qid=Q1373049|lt=Jingo-keiun|1=Jingo-keiun}} 1 (767) by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|qid=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|qid=Q13479654|lt=Chinjusha|1=Chinjusha}} for {{ill|Kosen-ji|ja|興禅寺 (河内長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosen-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosen-ji|1=Kozen-ji Temple (Kawachinagano)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11613444}} (formerly Darayama Jingūji) and as the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|qid=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} for {{ill|Nishikibe District|ja|錦部郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikibe District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikibe District|1=Nishigori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11650571}} in Kawachi Province. Initially named '''Shōhachimangū''', it has been affectionately referred to by locals as '''Kami-no-Miya''' (上宮) for generations.[http://www.kankou-kawachinagano.jp/blog/2017/06/post-824.html Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine - Kawachinagano Tourism Association Blog] Retrieved February 2, 2018. During the Meiji period, the shrine was renamed Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}}, separating it from Kosen-ji. Nearby shrines were merged into it, and it was designated a {{ill|Shinpenheihyakuryōkusha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpenheihyakuryōkusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpenheihyakuryōkusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. The main hall, built during the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}}, is said to contain a relic of Emperor Ōjin's hair. Despite the separation, the shrine retains vestiges of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} practices; visitors must pass through Kosen-ji’s precincts to reach the shrine. This unique layout, preserving elements of Shinto-Buddhist fusion, is a rare and valuable feature in Japan. == Visitor Information == ; location * 1-25-2 Mikanodai, {{ill|Kawachinagano|en|Kawachinagano|qid=Q819572|lt=Kawachinagano|1=Kawachinagano}}, Osaka Prefecture ; Access * Nearest station: Approximately 20 minutes on foot (about 1.4 km east) from {{ill|Mikanodai Station|en|Mikanodai Station|qid=Q6845717|lt=Mikanodai Station|1=Mikanodai Station}} on the {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|qid=Q71986|lt=Nankai Kōya Line|1=Koya Line}}. == References == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"|qq=}} === Citations === {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_08_kawatinagano_akasakauenoyama.html Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Akasakakaminoyama-jinja|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{pref-stub-Osaka|qq=}} ==List of Regional Soja== * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai-jingū|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|en|Nagao Shrine|qid=Q6958481|lt=Nagao Shrine|1=Nagao Shrine}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|qid=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine|1=Hotaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|Washinomiya Shrine|qid=Q611108|lt=Washinomiya Shrine|1=Washinomiya Shrine}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{shinto shrines|qq=}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Sōja shrines|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|赤坂上之山神社|version=85096222|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{everybodywiki link|Akasaka Uenoyama Shrine}} }}
